mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate directly reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. When FEDFUNDS falls below its 3-month moving average, equity risk premiums compress and S&P 500 forward returns increase over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.16% indicates near-zero rates
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances despite rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS 3-month growth rate and 60-day forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and forward economic contraction. This combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 412,403M and HOUST at 894K units
failure_condition: Negative forward returns occur in < 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 6-month trend indicates expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength. This supports margin expansion and positive equity returns over 5 trading days as market reprices supply-side resilience.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 52% of instances where INDPRO rises > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated unemployment (UNRATE) creates stagflation conditions. Central banks respond with rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month AND UNRATE > 6.5% AND FEDFUNDS < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 7.7% and FEDFUNDS at 0.16%
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the three-way interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized rate signals weakening aggregate demand and recession risk. This triggers equity repricing downward over 20 trading days as investors reduce growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 13,638.1 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising sharply (> 0.3pp in one month) signals labor market deterioration and forward earnings pressure. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity investors immediately reprice employment risk.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or shock event
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) combined with retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerating above their 6-month trend indicates strong consumer confidence and demand momentum. This joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise > 3% month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 894K and RSAFS at 412,403M
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with demand acceleration
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between HOUST and RSAFS is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).
